description: To plant seeds in prepared ground, initiating the cycle of growth. 'Thophe' is the act of faith that begins cultivation—placing potential into soil and trusting the process of germination, growth, and eventual harvest. Every sowing is a promise made to the future.
sound_symbolism: The word places gently like seeds into soil. 'Tho' /θo/ opens with dental breath, the careful tending motion. The round 'o' is the hole receiving seed. Then 'phe' /ɸe/ breathes life-potential, the seed placed, the future planted.
grammatical_notes: Functions as a transitive verb: 'mia thinoe thophe.' (I sow seeds.) Can specify location: 'mia mua thepalu thinoe thophe.' (I sow seeds in the garden.) Begins the agricultural cycle that 'muraki' (harvest) completes.

pillars.solarpunk-values: Sowing embodies investment in future abundance—the patient work of planting what others may harvest, thinking beyond immediate return.
pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Karma is often compared to sowing—actions planted now bear fruit later. 'Thophe' carries this understanding of cause ripening into effect.
